km.England: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Nicola出生在艾歐洛。她於1205年之前與Thomas Champflour在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Nicola出生在艾歐洛。她于1205年之前与Thomas Champflour在艾歐洛结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Nicola
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Italian: from the male personal name Nicola, from Greek Nikolaos (see Nicholas ).
German (mainly Saarland) and French (Alsace and Lorraine): variant of   Nicolai   or   Nicolas  .
In some cases also an American shortened (and altered) form of Greek Nikolas , its variant Nicolas , or of any of various derivatives of the personal name Nikola(o)s ‘Nicholas’. Compare Nicol .

variants.name: Nicoletti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Angelo, Antonio, Carmine, Dino, Dante, Domenica, Ezio, Federico, Fiore, Francesca, Francesco.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Nicoletto, a pet form of the male personal name Nicola .

variants.name: Nicolazzo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Marco, Francesco, Michelangelo, Salvatore.  Spanish Domingo, Jose, Pascual.
Italian: from the personal name Nicola + the perjorative suffix -azzo.

variants.name: Niccoli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Santo, Serafino, Silvio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a variant of the personal name   Nicola  .

variants.name: Nicora
meanings: Italian (mainly Liguria; Nicòra): variant of Nicola .

variants.name: Nicolia
meanings: Italian (Frosinone; Nicolìa): derivative of the personal name Nicola . This surname is rare in Italy.

variants.name: Nicolini
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Enrico, Reno, Antonio, Carmela, Francesca, Giancarlo, Luciano, Premo, Riccardo, Rocco, Sal, Salvatore.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Nicolino, a pet form of Nicola .

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: English (southwestern England and south Wales) and Dutch: from the personal name Nicholas (from Latin Nicolaus, from Greek Nikolaos, from nikān ‘to conquer’ + laos ‘people’). Forms with -ch- are due to hypercorrection (compare Anthony ). The name in various vernacular forms was popular among Christians throughout Europe in the Middle Ages, largely as a result of the fame of a 4th-century Lycian bishop, about whom a large number of legends grew up, and who was venerated in the Orthodox Church as well as the Catholic. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Nikolić (see Nikolic ), Greek Papanikolaou ‘(son of) Nicholas the priest’ and Nikolopoulos . Compare Nickolas .

History: The colonial official and revolutionary patriot Robert Carter Nicholas was from a prominent VA family on both sides. His father was a British navy surgeon who emigrated c. 1700 from Lancashire, England, to Williamsburg, VA.

variants.name: Nicolella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Amadeo, Domenic, Mario.
Italian: from a pet form of the male personal name   Nicola  .

variants.name: Nicoletta
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Rocco, Antonietta, Antonio, Carmelo, Carmine, Enrico, Nunziata, Pasquale, Raffaele, Sal, Salvatore.
Italian: from a pet form of the male personal name   Nicola  .

variants.name: Nicolo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Rocco, Antonio, Carmine, Decio, Donato, Enrico, Mauro, Pasquale, Salvatore, Vincenzo.  Spanish Diego, Demetrio, Fernando, Palmira.
Italian (southern; Nicolò): variant of Nicola .

In some cases possibly also an American shortened (and altered) form of Greek patronymics Nicolopoulos or Nikolopoulos , or of composite names used as patronymics, such as Nikologiannis ‘John son of Nicholas’, or of Bulgarian and Macedonian patronymic Nikolov .

en.lifeSummary: Nicola was born in Europe. She married Thomas Champflour before 1205, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Nicola est née à Europe. Elle a épousé Thomas Champflour avant 1205, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Nicola wurde in Europa geboren. Sie hat vor 1205 in Europa Thomas Champflour geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Nicola è nata il nel Europa. Lei sposato Thomas Champflour prima del 1205, nel Europa. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicolaはヨーロッパで生まれた。1205年以前にヨーロッパでThomas Champflourと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicola(은)는 우럽에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1205년 이전 우럽에서 Thomas Champflour(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Nicola nasceu em Europa. Ela casou-se com Thomas Champflour antes 1205, em Europa. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Nicola родилась в местности Европа. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Champflour перед 1205 г. в местности Европа. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Nicola nació en Europa. Se casó con Thomas Champflour antes de 1205, en Europa. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
